title: Creamy Sweet Vietnamese Salmon Soup
description: A silky, sweet Vietnamese-inspired soup featuring delicately flaked hotsmoked salmon, coconut milk, and aromatic herbs, perfect for a romantic dinner while keeping sugars low.

ingredients.name: Salmon fillet
ingredients.notes: Sous‑vide at 125°F (52°C) for 30 min, then flake

instructions: Vacuum‑seal the salmon fillet with a pinch of salt and pepper, then sous‑vide at 125°F (52°C) for 30 minutes. Remove, allow to cool, and gently flake the salmon, discarding skin and bones.
In a medium saucepan combine the vegetable broth, coconut milk, fish sauce, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and monk fruit sweetener. Stir until the sweetener dissolves.
Add the bruised lemongrass, sliced ginger, and minced garlic to the saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat and let the aromatics infuse for 8–10 minutes.
Churn the mixture to remove the lemongrass, ginger, and garlic, leaving a clear broth.
Return the broth to low heat, then fold in the flaked salmon. Warm the salmon through gently for 2–3 minutes, avoiding a rolling boil to preserve delicate flakes.
Taste and adjust seasoning with a splash of lime juice and, if needed, a few drops more fish sauce or soy sauce.
Ladle the soup into individual bowls, garnish with sliced scallions, a swirl of coconut milk, and serve with a lime wedge on the side.
Serve immediately, ideally paired with steamed jasmine rice or a crisp green salad.
